seoTitle: Point Piper vs Pointer Mountain — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Point Piper and Pointer Mountain. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Point Piper vs Pointer Mountain: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Point Piper and Pointer Mountain.
heading: Point Piper vs Pointer Mountain — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Point Piper, New South Wales and Pointer Mountain, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pointer Mountain has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Point Piper (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Point Piper or Pointer Mountain safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Point Piper has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Pointer Mountain scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Point Piper scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Pointer Mountain scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Point Piper or Pointer Mountain?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pointer Mountain has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
